item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Keeping your kitchen clean matters more than you think
item.description: THERE’S something undeniably comforting about walking into a sparkling-clean kitchen. The countertops are clutter-free, the sink is empty, and the entire space feels fresh and inviting.
item.content:encoded:
THERE’S something undeniably comforting about walking into a sparkling-clean kitchen.

The countertops are clutter-free, the sink is empty, and the entire space feels fresh and inviting.

While a clean kitchen certainly looks great, its benefits go far beyond appearance. Maintaining a clean kitchen is one of the smartest ways to protect your family’s health, reduce daily stress, and preserve the heart of your home.

At Sandia Green Clean, we know how much life happens in the kitchen.

From preparing meals and sharing family conversations to helping with homework at the table, the kitchen is often the busiest room in the house.

That’s why it deserves a little extra care and attention.

Here are five important reasons why keeping your kitchen clean should be a top priority.

1. A clean kitchen helps keep your family healthy:

Your kitchen may appear spotless, but harmful germs can hide in places you might not expect. Countertops, cutting boards, sink handles, and appliance surfaces can all harbor bacteria that can contaminate food and lead to illness.

In fact, studies have shown that kitchens often contain more bacteria than bathrooms due to the frequent handling of food. Regular deep cleaning helps eliminate dangerous bacteria such as E. coli and Salmonella, reducing the risk of foodborne illnesses.

Professional cleaners know where germs tend to hide and use effective cleaning methods to sanitize high-touch and hard-to-reach areas. With a professionally cleaned kitchen, you can prepare meals with greater confidence and peace of mind.

Even the smallest crumbs or spills can attract unwanted pests. Food residue under appliances, sticky spots on countertops, and overflowing trash cans can quickly become an invitation for ants, flies, cockroaches, and even rodents.

Routine kitchen cleaning removes the food sources and odors that pests seek out. Deep cleaning is especially important because it targets hidden areas that are often overlooked during everyday tidying.

At Sandia Green Clean, our eco-friendly cleaning approach helps create a sanitary environment that discourages pests naturally, keeping your kitchen fresh, clean, and free from unwanted visitors.

3. A tidy kitchen saves time and reduces stress:

Cooking in a cluttered or dirty kitchen can be frustrating. Searching for clean utensils, clearing counter space, and cleaning up old messes while trying to prepare a meal can make even simple tasks feel overwhelming.

A clean and organized kitchen makes meal preparation easier, faster, and more enjoyable. When everything is in its place, you can focus on cooking instead of cleaning.

Professional cleaning services help maintain that sense of order, allowing you to enjoy a more functional kitchen without adding another chore to your busy schedule.

4. It protects your appliances and surfaces:

Kitchen surfaces and appliances represent a significant investment in your home. Over time, grease buildup, food splatters, and acidic spills can damage countertops, stain surfaces, and reduce the lifespan of appliances.

Regular professional cleaning helps prevent long-term wear and tear by removing harmful residue before it causes damage. Using the right products is equally important.

Maintaining a clean kitchen impacts your physical health, mental clarity, and financial well-being.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Camari is a loyal customer
item.description: FOR nearly 30-years, 60-year-old Camari Serukula Rawatimo of Nakorosule, Naitasiri has been a loyal customer of RB Patel Supermarket, making frequent trips to Nausori and Suva to provide for her family’s household needs. Living in the highlands of Naitasiri, Mrs Rawatimo says shopping has become a skill she has mastered over the years.
item.content:encoded:
FOR nearly 30-years, 60-year-old Camari Serukula Rawatimo of Nakorosule, Naitasiri has been a loyal customer of RB Patel Supermarket, making frequent trips to Nausori and Suva to provide for her family’s household needs.

Living in the highlands of Naitasiri, Mrs Rawatimo says shopping has become a skill she has mastered over the years.

With experience comes wisdom, especially when it comes to budgeting, choosing quality products, and ensuring the family receives the best value for money.

“Shopping at this age is actually an advantage because we know what is best for our family,” she shared.

Although she mostly shops at the Nausori branch due to convenience, she also visits RB Patel Supermarket whenever she travels to the capital.

She says the excellent customer service, affordable prices, and wide variety of products continue to make RB Patel her trusted supermarket of choice.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Camari chooses RB Patel supermarket
item.description: FOR 60-year-old Camari Serukula Rawatimo of Nakorosule in Naitasiri, grocery shopping is more than just a weekly routine it is a responsibility she proudly carries for her entire family. Having spent almost 30-years shopping at RB Patel Supermarket, Rawatimo says the supermarket has become her trusted one-stop destination for quality groceries, affordable prices, and reliable customer service.
item.content:encoded:
FOR 60-year-old Camari Serukula Rawatimo of Nakorosule in Naitasiri, grocery shopping is more than just a weekly routine it is a responsibility she proudly carries for her entire family.

Having spent almost 30-years shopping at RB Patel Supermarket, Rawatimo says the supermarket has become her trusted one-stop destination for quality groceries, affordable prices, and reliable customer service.

Living in the highlands of Naitasiri, Rawatimo knows very well the challenges that come with travelling long distances to town for shopping.

Despite the difficulties, she continues to make regular trips to Nausori and Suva to ensure her family has everything they need at home.

“Shopping at this age is actually an advantage,” she said with a smile.

“We know what is best for our family, what products to buy, and how to budget properly. That is why I continue to choose RB Patel for all my grocery needs.”

Rawatimo usually shops at RB Patel Supermarket. Nausori because it is convenient for her when travelling from Naitasiri.

However, whenever she visits Suva, she also shops at RB Patel Supermarket, saying the quality of service remains consistent no matter which branch she visits.

“The customer service is always great, and the supermarket is usually full of shoppers every day.

“That shows people trust the products, the prices, and the variety available instore.”

Coming from a large family household, grocery shopping is a carefully planned task.

Every Friday, family members contribute money towards the weekly groceries, and Rawatimo takes on the responsibility of shopping for everyone.

“We all put together our shares for the groceries, and then I come and buy what the family needs.”

“It is important to shop wisely because life is becoming harder and prices are increasing.”

She said budgeting has become increasingly important as the cost of living continues to rise.

According to her, shoppers must learn to compare products, prioritise essential household items, and avoid unnecessary spending.

“Budgeting and planning your shopping is very important.

“You need to know what to buy and also choose the best products that will last for the family.”

Because travelling from the village to town is not always easy, Rawatimo ensures she writes a detailed shopping list before leaving home.

“In the village, it is difficult when we suddenly run out of something.

“That is why I make sure I write down everything before I leave, so I don’t forget anything important.”

The family usually does bulk shopping once every week. If they happen to run short of certain items by mid week, they rely on a nearby village canteen for smaller purchases.

Although shopping for a large family can sometimes be tiring, Rawatimo says she is grateful for the support she receives from her children, nephews, and nieces.

“I usually do the shopping alone, but sometimes my nephews or nieces come to help me,” she said.

“I’m thankful for my children because they still support and take care of me.”

After nearly three decades of loyalty, Rawatimo says her trust in RB Patel remains stronger than ever, proving that good service, quality products, and customer care can create lifelong shoppers.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | 106 years of service, savings and smiles
item.description: Courts Fiji is proud to celebrate an incredible milestone 106 years of serving Fijian families and communities. This year’s Birthday Promotion is set to be one of the biggest yet, bringing together unbeatable shopping value, exciting rewards, and the thrill of international football through the simultaneous launch of the Hisense FIFA World Cup TV Trade in Promotion. Under the campaign theme “Every
item.content:encoded:
Courts Fiji is proud to celebrate an incredible milestone 106 years of serving Fijian families and communities. This year’s Birthday Promotion is set to be one of the biggest yet, bringing together unbeatable shopping value, exciting rewards, and the thrill of international football through the simultaneous launch of the Hisense FIFA World Cup TV Trade in Promotion.

Under the campaign theme “Every Home Has a Courts Story,” the 106th Birthday Promotion recognizes the generations of customers who have made Courts a part of their homes and lives.

For over a century, Courts has remained committed to helping families create comfortable living spaces with quality furniture, appliances, electronics, and household essentials at affordable prices.

To mark this special occasion, customers can look forward to a range of exciting offers including Red Pen Deals, Combo Deals, F.O.C. Deals and Roadshow Gifts, and many more in-store surprises.

The promotion is designed to provide exceptional value while giving customers more reasons to celebrate with Courts.

Adding to the excitement is the launch of the Hisense FIFA World Cup Promotion, which combines world-class technology with football fever. As an Official FIFA World Cup Sponsor, Hisense continues to deliver innovative products that enhance the viewing experience for sports fans everywhere. Customers will have the opportunity to trade in their old televisions for brand-new Hisense models, making it easier than ever to upgrade their home entertainment setup ahead of the world’s biggest football tournament. Create the perfect living room set-up with our exquisite Signature Furniture overseas lounge collection, set up a bar fridge to keep your drinks cool while watching intense matches, make your popcorn with our range of microwaves so you do not have to miss a moment. Also, come in-store and check the range of sound bars and Hi-Fi systems to take your match day experience to the next level.

The celebration extends beyond great deals.

Every $50 spent at Courts during the promotional period will automatically qualify customers for a weekly prize draw, where 12 lucky families will each win a luxurious 2-night getaway for two at the DoubleTree Resort by Hilton Fiji – Sonaisali Island. The prize offers families the perfect opportunity to relax, reconnect, and create lasting memories in one of Fiji’s premier resort destinations.

The combined promotions reflect Courts’ ongoing commitment to rewarding loyal customers while bringing exciting international brands and experiences closer to Fijian households.

Whether customers are looking to furnish their homes, upgrade appliances, invest in the latest technology, or simply take advantage of outstanding savings, there has never been a better time to shop at Courts.

As Courts celebrates 106 years of trusted service, the organization thanks its customers for being part of the journey and looks forward to creating many more stories together.

Visit your nearest Courts store today and celebrate 106 years of value, innovation, and rewards because every home truly has a Courts story.

Hisense FIFA World Cup Promotion, combines world-class technology with football fever.

Far left: Courts celebrates 106 years of trusted service for the people of Fiji.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | The Flash Sale Is back | With bigger screens and bigger savings
item.description: The world’s biggest soccer tournament has arrived, and excitement is sweeping across Fiji. Families and friends are gathering to cheer every goal, celebrate every victory, and experience every unforgettable moment together. To make the occasion even more exciting, the Narhari Flash Sale is back with incredible offers on selected Panasonic and Aiwa televisions, giving you the perfect opportunity to
item.content:encoded:
The world’s biggest soccer tournament has arrived, and excitement is sweeping across Fiji.

Families and friends are gathering to cheer every goal, celebrate every victory, and experience every unforgettable moment together. To make the occasion even more exciting, the Narhari Flash Sale is back with incredible offers on selected Panasonic and Aiwa televisions, giving you the perfect opportunity to upgrade your home entertainment before the action begins.

For those who demand the very best viewing experience, Panasonic Mini LED 4K HDR Smart TVs deliver exceptional picture quality on the grandest stage.

Available in impressive 65″, 75″, and 86″ screen sizes, Panasonic’s advanced Mini LED technology produces deeper blacks, brighter highlights, and remarkable contrast that brings every match to life. From lightning-fast attacks to dramatic penalty shootouts, every movement appears smooth, sharp, and incredibly realistic, creating a true stadium atmosphere in your living room.

For families looking for outstanding performance and value, Panasonic 4K Smart Android LED TVs offer an ideal combination of stunning visuals and smart functionality.

Available in 50″, 65″, and 75″ models, these televisions deliver crystal-clear Ultra HD resolution, vibrant colour reproduction, and easy access to popular streaming platforms. Whether you are watching live soccer, catching up on highlights, or enjoying your favourite movies and series, Panasonic continues to provide the trusted quality and reliability that Fiji families have enjoyed for generations.

Adding even more excitement to this year’s Flash Sale is the arrival of the new Aiwa QLED TV range.

The Aiwa 55″ QLED TV is perfect for everyday entertainment, offering brilliant Quantum Dot colour, impressive 4K clarity, and a sleek modern design that complements any home. Moving up in size, the Aiwa 65″ QLED TV delivers a more immersive viewing experience with Dolby Audio, voice control, and smart webOS Hub technology that makes accessing content simple and enjoyable.

For those who want the ultimate home entertainment experience, the Aiwa 75″ QLED TV offers breathtaking screen size, vibrant colour performance, and exceptional picture quality that transforms every soccer match into an unforgettable spectacle.

This Soccer World Cup, do not settle for ordinary viewing. Bring home bigger screens, sharper pictures, richer colours, and smarter entertainment while enjoying unbeatable Flash Sale offers available only at Narhari. Every goal looks bigger. Every celebration feels louder. Every match becomes more memorable.

Visit your nearest Narhari branch today in:

Suva, Lautoka, Nadi, Ba, Yadua-Sigatoka, Rakiraki, Nausori, Navua, Savusavu, Nakasi and Labasa.

Visit our website: https://www.narharifiji.com/

Follow us for the latest deals, promos and tech updates:

: https://www.tiktok.com/@narhari_officialaccount

Impressive colour performance and stunning crisp motion clarity (Panasonic). Pictures:SUPPLIED

No more jumping from app to app (Panasonic).

Impressive sound that brings the stadium to you (Panasonic).

Step Into a Smarter World of Entertainment with AIWA webOS Hub TV. Picture: SUPPLIED

Discover the quantum leap in colour clarity and cinematic entertainment. Picture: SUPPLIED

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | VERE’S TAKE | Hidden inflation hits shoppers
item.description: Bula vinaka shoppers, a discussion in our newsroom recently turned to a topic that affects every household, the rising cost of groceries. Several colleagues shared stories about how much more they are paying for everyday essentials. Sophie mentioned that many products she regularly buys have increased by a dollar or more in a relatively short period of time. Items such as canned corned beef, chick
item.content:encoded:
Bula vinaka shoppers, a discussion in our newsroom recently turned to a topic that affects every household, the rising cost of groceries. Several colleagues shared stories about how much more they are paying for everyday essentials.

Sophie mentioned that many products she regularly buys have increased by a dollar or more in a relatively short period of time. Items such as canned corned beef, chicken, sausages, butter, fish, tea bags, bathing soap, eggs, potatoes, meat at the delies and washing powder as well as soap seem to be getting more expensive by the day, depending on where one shops.

Harold spoke about another challenge many families can relate to — planning affordable meals. With the prices of chicken and fish climbing steadily, he has been searching for alternative sources of protein to help stretch the family budget. One option he now considers is Nutrela Soya Chunks, a plant-based protein made from soy flour that is often used as a substitute for meat.

As food prices continue to rise, more households are looking at alternatives that can provide nutrition without putting additional pressure on already strained budgets, especially for families that live in the city. What many consumers are experiencing is now being backed by data.

The Consumer Council of Fiji recently released findings from a comprehensive market surveillance exercise conducted between late March and early May this year.

The study revealed that consumers are paying significantly more at supermarket checkouts, not only because of direct price increases but also due to a less obvious form of inflation.

According to the Council, Fiji is experiencing what can be described as “hidden inflation”. While shoppers often notice when shelf prices increase dramatically, a growing concern is the gradual disappearance of discounts many families have relied on to manage their weekly grocery bills.

Retailers are reducing the gap between regular prices and discounted prices.

In some cases, discounts have disappeared altogether.

This means that even when the shelf price appears unchanged, consumers end up paying more because the savings they once enjoyed are not available. The Council’s analysis identified what it described as a “two-tier” inflationary reality.

On one level, many commonly bought household items have experienced sustained price increases.

Products like rice, eggs, cookies, tea bags, bathing soap, sausages, washing powder, toilet paper and cooking oil recorded average price increases ranging from 15 to more than 35 per cent during the monitoring period.

On another level, consumers are losing access to the promotional discounts that previously softened the impact of rising prices.

This as Fiji continues to feel the effects of higher global fuel prices.

Fuel plays a critical role throughout the supply chain, affecting transportation, shipping, storage and distribution costs. When fuel costs rise, businesses often face increased operating expenses, many of which eventually find their way to consumers.

Produce at the market has all gone up and even price-controlled goods are not immune.

While these products remain below the maximum regulated prices, the average discounts offered on them have also declined. As a result, households are still paying more than they did previously. Consumer Council CEO Seema Shandil says the financial burden facing families is increasingly difficult to ignore.

She noted that the Council’s findings provide evidence of a creeping cost-of-living pressure that is not always noticed.

When discounts that families depend on are reduced, consumers effectively lose purchasing power even if the advertised retail price appears stable.

The Council has described this trend as a “discount illusion” — a situation where the retail environment appears unchanged, yet shoppers leave the checkout spending more money.

Another concern is what economists call the “staircase effect”. Prices move up one step at a time but rarely come back to previous levels.

Over time, this creates a new and more expensive baseline for the cost of living.

As global economic uncertainty continues and fuel prices remain volatile, there is a risk that the remaining discount buffers on staple goods could shrink even further, pushing grocery bills higher in the months ahead.

For now, the Consumer Council says it will continue monitoring supermarket prices.

In the meantime, consumers are encouraged to compare prices between stores.

Look carefully for genuine bargains, plan shopping trips in advance and stick to household budgets wherever possible.

For many families, every dollar matters.

As food and household costs continue to rise, smart shopping is not just a good habit.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: INDUSTRIAL MANUFACTURING | Building confidence | Using internationally recognised testing standards
item.description: Quality, accuracy and trust are fundamental in the steel industry, where reliable testing plays a critical role in ensuring product performance, safety and compliance. At BlueScope Pacific Steel Fiji, these values are reinforced through the accreditation of its Mechanical Test Laboratory by the International Accreditation New Zealand (IANZ).
item.content:encoded:
Quality, accuracy and trust are fundamental in the steel industry, where reliable testing plays a critical role in ensuring product performance, safety and compliance.

At BlueScope Pacific Steel Fiji, these values are reinforced through the accreditation of its Mechanical Test Laboratory by the International Accreditation New Zealand (IANZ).

Accreditation to ISO/IEC 17025:2017, the internationally recognised standard for testing and calibration laboratories, demonstrates that the laboratory has been independently assessed and recognised for its technical competence. This provides customers with confidence that testing is carried out accurately and consistently, producing results they can rely on when making important decisions about reinforcing steel quality and compliance.

A key strength of accredited laboratories is their use of validated testing methods and routine quality checks. These processes help ensure testing is performed consistently, reducing the risk of errors and improving confidence in the results.

Independent accreditation also offers greater assurance than internal reviews alone, as assessments are conducted by an external organisation against internationally accepted standards.

IANZ evaluates a range of factors, including staff competence, testing procedures, equipment calibration and quality management systems.

This rigorous assessment process helps ensure the laboratory continues to operate at a high standard and deliver reliable outcomes for customers.

Testing that falls within the laboratory’s accredited scope has been specifically assessed and approved by the accrediting body. This gives customers additional confidence that these services meet recognised requirements for quality and technical competence.

Skilled and experienced personnel are equally important in maintaining reliable testing.

Laboratory staff must follow approved procedures, maintain quality controls and identify potential issues before they affect results. Their expertise and attention to detail contribute significantly to the accuracy and consistency of testing outcomes.

Maintaining accreditation requires ongoing commitment. Equipment must be regularly calibrated and maintained, staff training kept up to date, records accurately managed and quality systems continually reviewed.

Regular surveillance visits and reassessments help ensure standards are maintained over time and support a culture of continuous improvement.

The international recognition of ISO/IEC 17025:2017 accreditation provides assurance that BlueScope Pacific Steel Fiji’s Mechanical Test Laboratory operates in line with globally accepted standards.

Through its commitment to quality, technical excellence and continuous improvement, the company continues to provide trusted testing services that support confidence in the quality and performance of its products.

Raquel Varea runs one of their tests. Pictures: MELI NAGUSUDRAU

Elisio Bulituraga and Raquel Varea are among the skilled professionals entrusted with overseeing the testing programme at BlueScope Pacific Steel Fiji, ensuring the highest standards of accuracy, reliability and quality are maintained throughout the testing process.

The BlueScope Pacific Steel Fiji testing lab is now internationally recognised for testing and calibration laboratories in Fiji.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Three decades of trust, quality and tradition
item.description: Golden range has become a trusted household staple name across Fiji. Producing a wide range of foods from margarine, spices and oils. Whether you are hosting a family gathering or celebrating with friends, Golden brands offer premium products that help you do it in style. Fiji Foods have been a part of the food industry for more than three decades. Located in Walu Bay, Suva, the business has grown
item.content:encoded:
Golden range has become a trusted household staple name across Fiji. Producing a wide range of foods from margarine, spices and oils. Whether you are hosting a family gathering or celebrating with friends, Golden brands offer premium products that help you do it in style.

Fiji Foods have been a part of the food industry for more than three decades. Located in Walu Bay, Suva, the business has grown into a trusted home brand. Known for producing edible oils including Soya bean, Canola, Mustard, Coconut oil & Vanaspati.

Additionally, Fiji Foods also manufacture and pack Golden margarine and Golden range spices for your everyday cooking.

Golden Spices are a popular product amongst the people of Fiji & the Pacific.

Having been locally sourced and produced, you are sure to taste the local blend of Fiji.

Their reach has also extended beyond Fiji, with the brand being present in international markets across Australia, New Zealand, and the Pacific.

Golden has been a part of everyday life in Fiji for more than three decades.

Being present in family meals, festive occasions, and commercial kitchens, symbolizing trust and tradition. For many households, Golden range is not just a cooking ingredient but a familiar Golden range, manufactured and distributed in Fiji by Motibhai Group – sharing quality, sharing happiness

Shainaaz Ali shops for Golden range od products at CJS supermarket, Nabua.

Golden range offers a wide range of cooking ingredients which can be used for many cooking purposes.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.
